claim_text: The adjacent market is converging on a 'control plane' / observability / governance framing for agent systems.
notes: Strongly supported by multiple first-party products in adjacent categories.

claim_text: Those adjacent control-plane products mostly optimize for agent telemetry, evaluation, governance, and fleet oversight rather than fine-grained human attention management.
notes: Inference from product surfaces and docs; they are adjacent but not direct substitutes for humanctl's core loop.

claim_text: User-facing agent systems need primitives such as shared state, interrupts, steering, streamed tool output, and multimodal attachments.
Agent-user products break the simple request/response model because agents are long-running, multi-turn, multimodal, and nondeterministic.
Human-centered agent interfaces benefit from co-planning, co-tasking, action approvals, and memory/plan learning rather than opaque autonomy.
notes: Strongly supported by Magentic-UI repo, blog, and report.

claim_text: The agent should interrupt the human as little as possible and only when necessary, especially for clarifying questions or help after failures.
notes: The exact policy is from Magentic-UI's design description; inference is that this should generalize to humanctl.

claim_text: Interruptions can preserve apparent speed and quality while still increasing stress, frustration, time pressure, and effort.
notes: Strong peer-reviewed evidence relevant to escalation design.

claim_text: Good human-AI UX should make capabilities legible, show relevant context, support efficient dismissal and correction, explain behavior, learn cautiously, and allow granular feedback.
notes: Supported by the Microsoft HAI guidelines summary and paper.

claim_text: A local-first product should treat the local copy as the primary source of truth and treat servers as secondary synchronization aids rather than the authority.
Long-running monitoring tasks create polling and context-overflow problems, so state must be persisted and resumable across long time windows.
notes: Strong support from Tell me When and Magentic-UI materials.

claim_text: Inbox and triage patterns are useful because they separate incoming attention items from the main workflow and support filtering, reminders, and review before commitment.
notes: Directly supported by Linear's docs; application to humanctl is an inference.

claim_text: Editable staged workflows are a better fit for human steering than opaque chat-only loops because humans can adjust specs, plans, and implementation directly.
notes: Supported across Magentic-UI and Copilot Workspace patterns.

claim_text: The hosted `humanctl.com` experience should be marketing plus sample/demo data; the real product runtime should be local-first and file-backed.
notes: Mostly analytical inference from local-first principles and long-running state needs.

claim_text: `humanctl` is best understood as a local mixed-initiative coordination layer for human-in-the-loop agent work, not as a generic artifact canvas or an observability dashboard.
notes: This is the central synthesis claim.

claim_text: V1 should center on asks, watches, artifacts, inbox/focus state, and resumable events, while excluding general infinite-canvas freedom, full observability, and cloud-first state.
notes: A synthesis claim translating evidence into product scope.
